posts.name: Zhenxuan Zhang
posts.text:
Dear experts,
I am following the tutorial in Combine [1] [1] and set up the CMSSW and Combine v10. But then when I try to go through the exercise, I realize I don’t have the pyroot in Python even after cmsenv. In previous versions like CMSSW_11*, it’s there right? Any idea how to fix this?
I really appreciate any help you can provide.
Zhenxuan
[1] https://cms-analysis.github.io/HiggsAnalysis-CombinedLimit/latest/#installation-instructions
posts.cooked_html:
Dear experts,

I am following the tutorial in Combine [1] [1] and set up the CMSSW and Combine v10. But then when I try to go through the exercise, I realize I don’t have the pyroot in Python even after cmsenv. In previous versions like CMSSW_11*, it’s there right? Any idea how to fix this?

I really appreciate any help you can provide.

Zhenxuan

[1] https://cms-analysis.github.io/HiggsAnalysis-CombinedLimit/latest/#installation-instructions

posts.name: Zhenxuan Zhang
posts.text:
the command are:
cmsrel CMSSW_14_1_0_pre4
cd CMSSW_14_1_0_pre4/src
cmsenv
git clone https://github.com/cms-analysis/HiggsAnalysis-CombinedLimit.git HiggsAnalysis/CombinedLimit
cd HiggsAnalysis/CombinedLimit
posts.cooked_html:
the command are:

cmsrel CMSSW_14_1_0_pre4
cd CMSSW_14_1_0_pre4/src
cmsenv
git clone https://github.com/cms-analysis/HiggsAnalysis-CombinedLimit.git HiggsAnalysis/CombinedLimit
cd HiggsAnalysis/CombinedLimit

posts.name: Zhenxuan Zhang
posts.text: I’m sorry for taking up your time; it was my mistake not to change the Python path. Now it’s fixed
posts.cooked_html: I’m sorry for taking up your time; it was my mistake not to change the Python path. Now it’s fixed
